prose: Fukui reactivity indices for benzaldehyde were computed using DFT with B3LYP/def2-TZVP in PySCF, gas phase, charge 0, multiplicity 1. Neutral state charges used Mulliken partitioning; f+/f-/dual indices characterize the response to oxidation/reduction. O atom (index 1): f- (most nucleophilic) = 0.2384; C atom (index 3): f+ (most electrophilic) = 0.3994. Trustworthiness was confirmed, but produced warnings: 'sum(f+) = 1.0000 (should be ~1.0; large drift suggests an N±1 state did not converge)', 'sum(f-) = 1.0000 (should be ~1.0; large drift suggests an N±1 state did not converge)'. For visualization and full insight, a diagram plot is accessible at /home/erobles/chem-skills/benchmarks/fidelity/fukui-reactivity-validation/benzaldehyde/argo_gpt-4o/20260704-051330_benzaldehyde_fukui_reactivity/agent_call_01_plot.png.
